mission: Sharpen the future APS/inflow route by reducing the geometric bulk datum Y under a boundary-locality condition.

theorem_1_s2_boundary_three_manifold_red.statement: Any compact connected oriented 3-manifold Y with boundary S^2 can be capped by a 3-ball to form a closed oriented 3-manifold M, so Y is diffeomorphic to M with one open 3-ball removed. Thus the boundary data always sit on a canonical collar/3-ball neighborhood of the boundary sphere.

formulas.boundary_collar: near-boundary geometry reduces to S^2 x [0,1]

theorem_2_boundary_local_inflow_depends_.statement: If the future eta-shift is boundary-local, meaning it depends only on a collar neighborhood of S^2 and a parity-odd bulk coupling, then the closed interior summand M_closed decouples from the boundary eta variation. Under that condition the inflow route reduces from the pair (Y, kappa_odd) to the single coupling datum kappa_odd on a canonical 3-ball/collar extension.

formulas.locality_hypothesis: delta eta depends only on collar(S^2 subset Y)
formulas.canonical_model: B^3 or S^2 x [0,1] with parity-odd bulk term

theorem_3_current_stack_value.statement: The present stack derives no parity-odd bulk coupling at all. Therefore even under the boundary-locality reduction, the current-stack value is kappa_odd = 0.

imports.inflow_boundary: The APS/anomaly-inflow route is fully reduced. On the current stack there is no hidden inflow object. Any future positive route must explicitly derive a parity-odd 3D extension pair (Y, kappa_odd) producing a nonzero boundary eta shift.

final_boundary.safe_statement: Under the natural boundary-locality hypothesis, the future inflow route reduces from (Y, kappa_odd) to the single parity-odd coupling datum kappa_odd on a canonical 3-ball/collar extension. On the current stack, kappa_odd = 0.
